heading: Medical and dental care: Academy cadets and midshipmen; members of, and designated applicants for membership in, Senior ROTC
label: 1074b Medical and dental care: Academy cadets and midshipmen; members of, and designated applicants for membership in, Senior ROTC
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1074b, added , , ; amended , , , which related to transitional medical and dental care for members on active duty in support of contingency operations, was repealed by , (d), , , with provision that the section, as in effect before , was to continue to apply to a member of the Armed Forces who was released from active duty in support of a contingency operation before that date.Pub. children.text: A cadet at the United States Military Academy, the United States Air Force Academy, or the Coast Guard Academy, and a midshipman at the United States Naval Academy, who incurs or aggravates an injury, illness, or disease in the line of duty.

children.label: A
children.text: in the line of duty while performing duties under ;section 2109 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: while traveling directly to or from the place at which that member or applicant is to perform or has performed duties pursuant to ; orsection 2109 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: in the line of duty while remaining overnight immediately before the commencement of duties performed pursuant to or, while remaining overnight, between successive periods of performing duties pursuant to , at or in the vicinity of the site of the duties performed pursuant to , if the site is outside reasonable commuting distance from the residence of the member or designated applicant.section 2109 of this titlesection 2109 of this titlesection 2109 of this title

the medical and dental care under this chapter that is appropriate for the treatment of the injury, illness, or disease until the injury, illness, disease, or any resulting disability cannot be materially improved by further hospitalization or treatment; and

children.heading: .—Exception for Gross Negligence or Misconduct
children.label: c .—Exception for Gross Negligence or Misconduct
children.text: A person is not entitled to benefits under subsection (b) for an injury, illness, or disease, or the aggravation of an injury, illness, or disease that is a result of the gross negligence or the misconduct of that person.
